intent: have legal cover in place to start the project Friday
notes: Necessity / dissolution (algorithm-spec §8.2, propose-and-log default). The happy path treats 'obtain a signed contract' as an instrumental sub-goal, but every signed-contract method tombstones (standard contract, redline the clause, their template, counter-sign). A signed contract was only an ASSUMED method: the PARENT desire 'legal cover to start' is reachable another way that BYPASSES the sub-goal — a letter of intent or a purchase order — which progresses to success via 'verify'. Expected: once the contract methods are exhausted, the planner RECORDS a dispensability/necessity judgement (propose-and-log, NOT a silent prune), climbs, and reaches legal cover via the bypass. Fully side-effect-free: every outcome comes from a rule or the tombstone default; no rule runs a real action.

rules.reason: vendor rejects a clause in our standard contract
the redlined clause is still rejected
their template adds an unacceptable indemnity clause
counter-signature stalls in their legal queue past Friday
no signature is reachable before Friday
a letter of intent gives legal cover to start
a purchase order gives legal cover to start
legal cover confirmed in place to start Friday
